aggregate_root_scheme: sha256 over canonical sorted list of {case_id, commitment_hash}

opentimestamps.btc_confirmation: expected within ~1-6 hours of submission timestamp

verification_three_step: 2. Confirm Rekor sealed exactly that hash: rekor-cli get --uuid=108e9186e8c5677ab7794690c9d81eab1b813414af1088223a57766e7a2ae8c84cac1bcc7b4b3506
3. Verify OpenTimestamps once Bitcoin confirms (~1-6h after submission): ots verify anchor-payload-eu-v2.json.ots -f anchor-payload-eu-v2.json

calibration_delta_vs_eu_v1.schema: rule_id_set added in eu-v2 by case
calibration_delta_vs_eu_v1.explanation: eu-v2 is purely additive on eu-v1. The eu-v1 rule effects (EU-PLD-03 black-box presumption, EU-PLD-04 disclosure-floor, EU-PLD-05 joint-and-several recoverable redistribution) all still fire. v2 adds PLD 2024/2853 Art. 7(c)/(d)/(e)/(f) defect axes, Art. 12(2) SME software-component recourse waiver, AI Act Art. 25 value-chain shift, Art. 26 monitoring-failure floor, Art. 27 FRIA, CJEU C-634/21 SCHUFA Art. 22 expansion, CJEU C-300/21 non-material damages, DSA Art. 6 hosting-provider knowledge, and 4 member-state PLD transpositions (FR/DE/IT/ES).

predictions.case_name: Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens v. Clearview AI Inc. — €30.5M (eu-v2)
predictions.agreement_with_regulator: Engine matches AP on primary defendant. PLD-V2-03 (post-market manufacturer-control) lifts Clearview attributable share; SCHUFA C-634/21 expansion engages because LE customers "draw strongly on" the matching outputs.
Engine matches Garante on primary defendant (Luka). PLD-V2-03 + MS-V2-IT (Italian Codice del Consumo Arts. 114, 117 strict-liability presumption) lift Luka recoverable share above 28% floor.

Engine matches AEPD on primary defendant. MS-V2-ES (Spanish TRLGDCU Arts. 135, 137, 138 reverse-burden) floors Tools for Humanity attributable at 50% and recoverable at 30%; PLD-05 redistributes residual recoverable to EU-resident orb operators + data subjects.
